hypothesis: In MADE episodes on held-out ternary and quaternary chemical systems initialized with Materials Project hulls, a multi-fidelity agent that uses calibrated epistemic uncertainty from an MLIP to drive an information-gain acquisition function and selectively allocates a fixed budget of high-fidelity DFT queries to high-uncertainty candidates will discover more novel thermodynamically stable compounds per DFT query than agents using diversity-only or uncertainty-agnostic selection.
